steps.title: Test all isosceles configurations with angle A = 50°
steps.content:
In an isosceles triangle, two angles must be equal. Since A = 50°, there are three possible configurations:
  • A = B = 50°, so C = 80°
  • A = C = 50°, so B = 80°
  • B = C, so 2B + 50° = 180°, giving B = 65°
The possible values for angle B are: 50° (choice A), 80° (choice C), and 65° (choice B). Choice D is 100°, which cannot occur when triangle ABC is isosceles with A = 50°. That matches choice D — D is correct.
